23.reason: Error: Unconditionally prints a decimal point. Should print dot only if there are more digits after ans[st] (i.e., if st+1 < ttt). Otherwise, it should print just the digit without dot (e.g., for "0.01" expected "1e-2", but prints "1.e-2").

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Unconditionally prints a decimal point. Should print dot only if there are more digits after ans[st] (i.e., if st+1 < ttt). Otherwise, it should print just the digit without dot (e.g., for "0.01" expected "1e-2", but prints "1.e-2").
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Always prints a decimal point even when there are no digits after the base digit (e.g., for "0.1" it produces "1." instead of "1"). Should only print '.' if there are remaining digits.

56.reason: Error: Similar issue as above. Unconditionally prints a decimal point after the first non-zero digit. Should print dot only if there are more digits (i+1 < ttt), otherwise just the digit without dot.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Similar issue as above. Unconditionally prints a decimal point after the first non-zero digit. Should print dot only if there are more digits (i+1 < ttt), otherwise just the digit without dot.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Same issue as above – unconditionally adds a decimal point, producing "-1." instead of "-1" when no digits follow (e.g., "-0.1").

6.reason: gets() has been removed from the C11 standard and causes a compilation error in modern compilers. Use fgets() or scanf() instead.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  gets() has been removed from the C11 standard and causes a compilation error in modern compilers. Use fgets() or scanf() instead.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Use of `gets()` is dangerous and removed from C11 standard; causes compilation error in strict environments. Should use `fgets()` instead.]
